checklist_note: 31 items generated across 2 batches (dedup dropped 2 registry string-presence dups). Lite probes (registry string-presence + JSON validity) verified directly and PASS. Remaining agent-mode items were factual contract claims about the analyzer/wfr API and the ACs; the Phase-3 review agents (esp. code-reviewer, silent-failure-hunter, final-pass) independently exercised those same absolute_claim / dependency_interaction surfaces and surfaced the real defects, which drove the fixes below.

phase3_findings.description: gh api calls omit --method GET; gh POSTs to GET-only Actions endpoints; exporter never fetches a run
--field=created<= splits on first '=' -> unknown key 'created<'; upper bound silently dropped; contradicts 'closed window'
cancel/interrupt/abort regex on raw stdout before terminal check drops successful launches from counts
load_cloud_mappings silent {} on malformed section -> all cloud jobs ineligible but census 'available'; no breadcrumb
malformed-manifest surviving source_status untested; masks the join clobber (corroborates SFH)
_workspace_state loose substrings; 'tracked' matches inside 'untracked'; can flip coverage to complete
_cleanup swallows unlink failures, reports success while sensitive 0600 artifacts survive
exit-code regex bare 'exit'/'rc' alternatives can bind wrong number
new 5th Python gh-caller (export script) outside run.sh DEVFLOW_GH pin loop + CLAUDE.md roster
dataclasses model closed enums / structured sub-records as bare str/dict, enforce nothing at construction (BindingIdentity secret coupling, EligibleLifecycle discriminated union, WorkspaceState)
join_confidence() and _validate_admitted_path post-resolve symlink branch read as guards that don't do what they appear to (dead)

fix_decisions.claim_text: exit-code regex bare exit/rc alternatives can bind wrong number
fix_decisions.evidence: Real but low-impact: regex requires the token immediately adjacent to a digit (exit\s*[:=]?\s*\d), so common false positives like 'exited 5' do not match; blast radius is only the terminal-vs-result-missing split. Suggestion-level, not fixed this iteration.

fix_decisions.claim_text: new 5th Python gh-caller outside run.sh DEVFLOW_GH pin loop + CLAUDE.md 'four Python gh-callers' roster
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion-level. The export script DOES correctly honor DEVFLOW_GH (os.environ.get('DEVFLOW_GH') or 'gh'), so no behavioral defect — only a missing pin + a stale CLAUDE.md roster count. The CLAUDE.md roster is not in this PR's diff (widening the surface), and a mid-loop CLAUDE.md edit is routing-restricted; noted for a follow-up.

Test-coverage expansion beyond the fixes; the guard itself is unchanged by this PR's own logic. Valuable follow-up (criticality 8 per pr-test-analyzer) but out of this fix loop's scope-bounded surface.

fix_decisions.claim_text: dataclasses enforce closed enums / sub-record shapes only via factories, not __post_init__
fix_decisions.evidence: Advisory design hardening. Production paths route through the factories (_binding_identity, build_local_census); adding __post_init__ asserts risks breaking the tests that construct instances directly for mutation testing. Wave-1 design tradeoff.

fix_decisions.claim_text: join_confidence() tests-only; _validate_admitted_path symlink branch dead after .resolve()
fix_decisions.evidence: Dead-but-harmless. Not fixed to avoid churn; noted for a future cleanup.

step_2_5_note: All findings routed to the fixer are codebase claims (pass straight through) except the two gh-behavior claims (F1/F2), which are external-tool claims verified against `gh api --help` (authoritative doc): 'adding request parameters will automatically switch the request method to POST ... use --method GET'. Confirmed. No findings refuted.

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty (grades committed HEAD); runs clean in CI on the committed tree

fix_delta_gate.note: One Suggestion accepted as advisory: F3 assumes a genuine cancellation always carries is_error=True (matches Claude Code interrupt behavior; residual failure direction over-counts a launch rather than dropping one — the safe direction).
